description:
New command. bl remaster <target>: set state_remote (local override by default; --commit writes the project-wide committed config), git fetch the target's balls/tasks, then reconcile: replay local-only task files onto the fetched branch (disjoint JSON files merge under stock git per SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md S2.5; the id-collision retry renames the rare true hash clash on import) and reset the state worktree to the reconciled branch. Idempotent: re-running against the same target is a no-op fetch. --detach: fork the current shared branch into a fresh local orphan and clear state_remote (repo becomes standalone again).

This is the entire recovery surface the user asked for: wrong hub -> remaster again; standalone repo joining late -> remaster reconciles; requirements change -> remaster --detach; hub moved -> remaster <new-url>.

WHY: recovery must be a first-class verb, not manual git surgery, because "stable if someone inits unaware" requires a non-destructive path back INTO a project after an independent init. Reconcile is the load-bearing part: it is what makes an unaware bl init safe rather than merely tolerated.
title: bl remaster: re-point the task state branch, with reconcile and --detach
